description:
To properly set the owner of /var/log/*.journal(~), run the command: 
$ sudo chown root /var/log/*.journal(~)
rationale: The <tt>/var/log/*.journal(~)</tt> files are system logs managed by the "systemd" service.
ocil_clause: /var/log/*.journal(~) does not have an owner of root
ocil:
To check the ownership of /var/log/*.journal(~), run the command: 
$ ls -lL /var/log/*.journal(~)
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following owner: root
fixtext:
To properly set the owner of /var/log/*.journal(~), run the command: 
$ sudo chown root /var/log/*.journal(~)
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /var/log/*.journal(~) file must be owned by root.
title: Verify User Who Owns /var/log/*.journal(~) Files
